evals.expected_output: Security audit identifying SQL injection vulnerability (no parameterized query), missing authentication/authorization check (anyone can fetch any user's profile = IDOR), and potentially returning sensitive fields in SELECT *.
expectations: Report identifies SQL injection vulnerability (OWASP A03:2021)
Report identifies missing authorization check / IDOR vulnerability (OWASP A01:2021)
At least one finding is rated Critical or High
Report provides parameterized query as the SQL injection fix
Report provides corrected code for at least the SQL injection issue

evals.expected_output: Critical security audit finding: jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(), meaning the JWT signature is never validated. Anyone can craft a JWT with any payload and bypass authentication. Also should flag token in query string (logged in URLs) and missing algorithm specification.
expectations: Report identifies the jwt.decode vs jwt.verify issue as Critical severity
Report references authentication failure category (OWASP A07:2021)
Report provides corrected code using jwt.verify() with secret
Report notes the risk of token being passed as a query parameter
Report flags the lack of algorithm specification as a security concern

evals.prompt: Can you do a security review of our React frontend password reset flow? When a user forgets their password, we email them a link like: https://app.com/reset?token=abc123&email=user@example.com. The token is valid for 7 days. The backend checks if the token matches the email in the database.
evals.expected_output: Security review covering: tokens valid for too long (7 days), email in URL leaks to server logs/referrers (Referer header), tokens should be invalidated after use, should use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks, rate limiting on reset endpoint.
expectations: Report flags 7-day token expiry as excessive (recommend 1 hour or similar)
Report identifies email address in URL as a privacy/logging risk
Report recommends single-use tokens (invalidate after successful reset)
At least 3 distinct findings are identified
Severity levels are assigned to each finding
